resona-ai.content:
Resona AI¶

Resona is Schema Weaver's AI assistant. It is embedded in two products — the SQL Editor and the Data Explorer — each with a purpose-built architecture for its context. Both surfaces use the same model selection, the same credit system, and the same account.

Two Products, One AI¶

Resona in the SQL Editor¶

The SQL Editor's Resona AI is an agentic schema assistant. It has access to your project files, your compiled schema graph, your ER diagram, and 55 purpose-built tools for writing DDL, analyzing schema quality, editing files surgically across multiple files, diffing and applying database migrations, auditing security and performance, and more.

It behaves like an AI pair-programmer for your database schema — it can read your files, reason about your schema graph, make targeted multi-file edits, and apply migrations to a live database. All from a conversation.

→ Full documentation: Resona AI in the SQL Editor

Resona in the Data Explorer¶

The Data Explorer's Resona AI is a live data analysis assistant. It connects to your running PostgreSQL database and answers questions about your actual data — generating and executing SQL queries, producing charts and visualizations, running statistical analysis, building reports, and exporting results.

It understands your schema but works with live rows — you ask business questions and get answers, not DDL.

→ Full documentation: Resona AI in the Data Explorer

Comparing the Two¶

SQL Editor AI Data Explorer AI
What it works with Schema definitions (DDL), project files Live database rows, query results
Primary output SQL DDL, file edits, migration scripts Query results, charts, reports, exports
Architecture Agentic loop with 55 schema tools Agentic loop with data analysis tools
Database required Optional (needed only for migrations/drift) Required (queries run against live data)
Applies changes Yes — writes to your SQL files, applies migrations Read-only — only SELECT queries
Charts No Yes — bar, line, scatter, pie, time-series
Exports SQL files, Prisma, Drizzle, DBML, TypeScript CSV, Excel, PDF reports, PPTX decks

Use the SQL Editor AI when designing or evolving your schema. Use the Data Explorer AI when you need to query and analyze live data.

How Resona Works¶

Both surfaces use a ReAct agentic loop — Resona does not just generate a single response. It plans, calls tools, observes results, and iterates until it has a complete answer:

Your message
     ↓
Intent classification
     ↓
Context assembly (schema / live data / history)
     ↓
  ┌── THINK → CALL TOOLS → OBSERVE ──┐
  └──────────── (iterate) ────────────┘
     ↓
Stream final answer + tool trace

You can watch this process in real time — every tool call is shown in the chat with its name and result, so you always know what Resona is doing.

Privacy¶

Resona's architecture is schema-first, not data-first:

What is sent to the AI model What stays on your infrastructure
Table names, column names, types Actual row data
Constraints, foreign keys, indexes Credentials and secrets
Your natural-language question Query results
Schema structure and relationships Backups and snapshots

Your row-level data never leaves your infrastructure. The AI model receives only schema metadata and your question. Queries are executed locally against your database connection.

Shared Credits¶

Credits are shared across both surfaces — a conversation in the SQL Editor and a query in the Data Explorer both draw from the same monthly pool. This means you have full flexibility: use Resona wherever it's most useful without managing separate budgets.

See Models and Credits for plan details and credit costs.

Getting Started¶

In the SQL Editor¶

  1. Open sql.schemaweaver.vivekmind.com
  2. Open a project and click Resona AI in the left sidebar
  3. Ask anything about your schema: "What indexes am I missing?" or "Create a users table with auth fields"

→ SQL Editor AI full guide

In the Data Explorer¶

  1. Open data.schemaweaver.vivekmind.com
  2. Select a database connection
  3. Click the Resona AI tab and start asking questions about your data

→ Data Explorer AI full guide

Next Steps¶

  • AI Chat Guide — tips for writing effective prompts and getting the best results from both surfaces
  • Models and Credits — model tiers (FAST → POWERFUL), credit costs, and plan limits
  • SQL Editor AI — agentic schema assistant with 55 tools
  • Data Explorer AI — live data analysis, charts, and reports

# Resona AI

Resona is Schema Weaver's AI assistant. It is embedded in two products — the **SQL Editor** and the **Data Explorer** — each with a purpose-built architecture for its context. Both surfaces use the same model selection, the same credit system, and the same account.

---

## Two Products, One AI

### Resona in the SQL Editor

The SQL Editor's Resona AI is an **agentic schema assistant**. It has access to your project files, your compiled schema graph, your ER diagram, and 55 purpose-built tools for writing DDL, analyzing schema quality, editing files surgically across multiple files, diffing and applying database migrations, auditing security and performance, and more.

![Resona AI open in the SQL Editor showing the agentic chat interface with tool execution and schema context](/images/sql-editor/resona-ai.png)

It behaves like an AI pair-programmer for your database schema — it can read your files, reason about your schema graph, make targeted multi-file edits, and apply migrations to a live database. All from a conversation.

→ [Full documentation: Resona AI in the SQL Editor](/sql-editor/ai-assistant)

---

### Resona in the Data Explorer

The Data Explorer's Resona AI is a **live data analysis assistant**. It connects to your running PostgreSQL database and answers questions about your actual data — generating and executing SQL queries, producing charts and visualizations, running statistical analysis, building reports, and exporting results.

![Resona AI open in the Data Explorer showing the natural-language chat interface with query results and chart output](/images/data-explorer/resona-ai.png)

It understands your schema but works with live rows — you ask business questions and get answers, not DDL.

→ [Full documentation: Resona AI in the Data Explorer](/data-explorer/ai-overview)

---

## Comparing the Two

| | SQL Editor AI | Data Explorer AI |
|---|---|---|
| **What it works with** | Schema definitions (DDL), project files | Live database rows, query results |
| **Primary output** | SQL DDL, file edits, migration scripts | Query results, charts, reports, exports |
| **Architecture** | Agentic loop with 55 schema tools | Agentic loop with data analysis tools |
| **Database required** | Optional (needed only for migrations/drift) | Required (queries run against live data) |
| **Applies changes** | Yes — writes to your SQL files, applies migrations | Read-only — only SELECT queries |
| **Charts** | No | Yes — bar, line, scatter, pie, time-series |
| **Exports** | SQL files, Prisma, Drizzle, DBML, TypeScript | CSV, Excel, PDF reports, PPTX decks |

Use the **SQL Editor AI** when designing or evolving your schema. Use the **Data Explorer AI** when you need to query and analyze live data.

---

## How Resona Works

Both surfaces use a **ReAct agentic loop** — Resona does not just generate a single response. It plans, calls tools, observes results, and iterates until it has a complete answer:

```
Your message
     ↓
Intent classification
     ↓
Context assembly (schema / live data / history)
     ↓
  ┌── THINK → CALL TOOLS → OBSERVE ──┐
  └──────────── (iterate) ────────────┘
     ↓
Stream final answer + tool trace
```

You can watch this process in real time — every tool call is shown in the chat with its name and result, so you always know what Resona is doing.

### Privacy

Resona's architecture is schema-first, not data-first:

| What is sent to the AI model | What stays on your infrastructure |
|---|---|
| Table names, column names, types | Actual row data |
| Constraints, foreign keys, indexes | Credentials and secrets |
| Your natural-language question | Query results |
| Schema structure and relationships | Backups and snapshots |

**Your row-level data never leaves your infrastructure.** The AI model receives only schema metadata and your question. Queries are executed locally against your database connection.

---

## Shared Credits

Credits are shared across both surfaces — a conversation in the SQL Editor and a query in the Data Explorer both draw from the same monthly pool. This means you have full flexibility: use Resona wherever it's most useful without managing separate budgets.

See [Models and Credits](/resona-ai/models) for plan details and credit costs.

---

## Getting Started

### In the SQL Editor

1. Open [sql.schemaweaver.vivekmind.com](https://sql.schemaweaver.vivekmind.com/)
2. Open a project and click **Resona AI** in the left sidebar
3. Ask anything about your schema: *"What indexes am I missing?"* or *"Create a users table with auth fields"*

→ [SQL Editor AI full guide](/sql-editor/ai-assistant)

### In the Data Explorer

1. Open [data.schemaweaver.vivekmind.com](https://data.schemaweaver.vivekmind.com/)
2. Select a database connection
3. Click the **Resona AI** tab and start asking questions about your data

→ [Data Explorer AI full guide](/data-explorer/ai-overview)

---

frontmatter.title: Models and Credits
frontmatter.description: All AI models available in Resona — four tiers (FAST, SMART, ADVANCED, POWERFUL), credit costs per model, plan limits, and how to choose the right model

headings.title: What You Can Do With Each Plan

resona-ai/models.content:
Models and Credits¶

Resona AI supports a large range of models from multiple providers, organized into four capability tiers. You choose the model per message — use a FAST model for quick questions and a POWERFUL model for complex multi-table design or deep analysis. Credits are shared across the SQL Editor and Data Explorer.

Model Tiers¶

Models are grouped into four tiers based on capability and cost. The credit multiplier shown in the UI (e.g. 1.0×) is relative to the base rate of the most commonly used FAST model.

FAST¶

The fastest and most cost-effective models. Best for everyday tasks — simple questions, quick lookups, iterative refinement, and high-volume workflows.

Model Provider Relative cost
GPT-4o Mini OpenAI 1.0× (base)
Claude Haiku Anthropic ~0.75×
Gemini 2.5 Flash Lite Google ~0.75×
Amazon Nova Lite AWS Bedrock ~0.75×
Qwen 3 AWS Bedrock ~0.75×

When to use FAST:

  • Simple schema questions or quick SQL generation
  • Iterating on a schema design across many messages
  • Any task where speed matters more than depth

SMART¶

A step up in reasoning ability, still very cost-efficient. Good for multi-table analysis and moderately complex questions.

Model Provider Relative cost
Gemini 2.5 Flash Google ~3×
DeepSeek V3 AWS Bedrock ~3.5×
Amazon Nova Pro AWS Bedrock ~3×
Qwen 3 Coder AWS Bedrock ~3×
Mistral Magistral AWS Bedrock ~3.5×

When to use SMART:

  • Multi-table JOIN queries with 3–5 tables
  • Schema analysis with moderate complexity
  • Coding-heavy tasks (Qwen Coder is specialized for code)

ADVANCED¶

High-capability models from the leading providers. Handles complex multi-table schemas, deep reasoning, and large context windows.

Model Provider Relative cost
GPT-4o OpenAI ~10×
GPT-4.1 OpenAI ~10×
Claude Sonnet Anthropic ~10×
Gemini 2.5 Pro Google ~12×
Mistral Large AWS Bedrock ~10×

When to use ADVANCED:

  • Complex multi-table schema design
  • Security audits and performance analysis
  • Large schemas (50–200 tables)
  • When SMART models give incorrect or incomplete results
  • Deep reasoning about schema architecture

POWERFUL¶

The most capable models available. Reserved for the most demanding tasks — very large schemas, complex agentic workflows, or when only the best reasoning will do.

Model Provider Relative cost
Claude Opus Anthropic ~56×
Kimi K2 Thinking AWS Bedrock ~28×

When to use POWERFUL:

  • Extremely large schemas (200+ tables)
  • Full-schema refactoring across many files
  • Complex multi-step migration planning
  • When ADVANCED models are not getting it right
  • Deep security or performance audits on production schemas

POWERFUL models are significantly more expensive. Use them selectively — most tasks are handled well by ADVANCED or even SMART.

Switching Models¶

The model selector is in the chat footer of both the SQL Editor and Data Explorer AI panels. You can switch models at any point — the change takes effect on the next message you send. Switching mid-conversation is fully supported.

The footer shows the active model name and its credit multiplier (e.g. Claude Sonnet 10×). This tells you how many credits the next message will consume relative to the base rate.

Credit System¶

Credits are the unit of AI usage in Schema Weaver. Every message you send to Resona consumes credits based on the model you selected and the amount of text processed (input + output tokens).

What Costs Credits¶

Action Credit cost
Sending a message to Resona Yes — based on model × tokens
Follow-up messages in the same conversation Yes
Re-running a saved query (no AI) No
Viewing query history No
Starting a new conversation No
Schema indexing No

Credit Multipliers¶

Credits are calculated per token processed, scaled by the model's tier multiplier. The exact cost per message depends on the length of your question, the schema context included, and the length of the response — but relative costs between models follow the tier multipliers shown above.

A longer schema context means more input tokens and therefore more credits per message, regardless of model.

Plans¶

Credits are shared between the SQL Editor AI and the Data Explorer AI. Usage in either surface draws from the same monthly pool.

Plan Monthly credits Daily limit Models available
Free 5,000 500 FAST only
Starter 100,000 10,000 FAST + SMART
Pro 200,000 20,000 FAST + SMART + ADVANCED
Power 433,000 43,300 All tiers (FAST → POWERFUL)

Credits reset on the 1st of every calendar month. Unused credits do not roll over to the next month.

The daily limit is 10% of the monthly allowance — this prevents the full monthly budget from being consumed in a single day.

What You Can Do With Each Plan¶

Free (5,000 credits/month):

  • ~5,000 messages at FAST model rates
  • Good for occasional schema questions and exploration
  • Only FAST tier models available

Starter (100,000 credits/month):

  • ~100,000 messages at FAST rates or ~28,000 at SMART rates
  • Suitable for regular daily use with SMART models
  • Access to FAST and SMART tier models

Pro (200,000 credits/month):

  • ~200,000 messages at FAST rates or ~20,000 at ADVANCED rates
  • Full access to ADVANCED models including GPT-4o and Claude Sonnet
  • Recommended for professional schema work and data analysis

Power (433,000 credits/month):

  • Access to all models including POWERFUL tier (Claude Opus, Kimi K2 Thinking)
  • For teams or individuals doing large-scale schema work or intensive data analysis

Checking Usage¶

Go to Settings → Usage to see a breakdown of credits used this month, remaining credits, per-model usage, and daily consumption.

On team plans, admins can view per-member usage breakdowns and optionally set per-member credit limits.

Credit Top-ups¶

If you reach your monthly limit before the cycle resets, you can purchase additional credits from Settings → Billing → Add Credits. Top-up credits are consumed first (before your monthly allocation) and do not expire at month-end.

FAQ¶

Are credits shared between SQL Editor and Data Explorer? Yes. Both surfaces draw from the same monthly credit pool on your account.

What happens when I run out of credits? A warning appears at 80% usage. At 100%, Resona falls back to FAST-tier models only (or stops, depending on your plan setting). You can purchase top-up credits or wait for the monthly reset.

Can multiple team members share a credit pool? Yes. On Pro and Power plans, credits are pooled across the workspace. Team admins can set per-user limits in Settings → Team → Credit Limits.

Can I use my own API keys? Yes. On Pro and Power plans you can provide your own OpenAI or Anthropic API keys (Settings → Resona AI → API Keys). BYOK queries are billed directly to your provider account and do not consume Schema Weaver credits.

What is the context window? Each model has its own context window — the amount of text (schema context + conversation history + your question) it can process at once. For large schemas, models with larger windows handle more tables in a single message. Claude models support 200K tokens; GPT-4o supports 128K; Gemini 2.5 Pro supports 1M tokens.

Next Steps¶

  • Resona AI Overview — what Resona is and how the two surfaces differ
  • AI Chat Guide — writing effective prompts for both surfaces
  • SQL Editor AI — the full 55-tool agentic assistant for schema design
  • Data Explorer AI — live data analysis, charts, and automated reports

    path: /resona-ai
  - title: AI Chat Guide
    path: /resona-ai/ai-chat
---

# Models and Credits

Resona AI supports a large range of models from multiple providers, organized into four capability tiers. You choose the model per message — use a FAST model for quick questions and a POWERFUL model for complex multi-table design or deep analysis. Credits are shared across the SQL Editor and Data Explorer.

---

## Model Tiers

Models are grouped into four tiers based on capability and cost. The credit multiplier shown in the UI (e.g. `1.0×`) is relative to the base rate of the most commonly used FAST model.

![The Resona AI model selector showing POWERFUL, ADVANCED, SMART, and FAST tier groups with models listed in each](/images/resona-ai/model.png)

---

### FAST

The fastest and most cost-effective models. Best for everyday tasks — simple questions, quick lookups, iterative refinement, and high-volume workflows.

| Model | Provider | Relative cost |
|---|---|---|
| GPT-4o Mini | OpenAI | 1.0× (base) |
| Claude Haiku | Anthropic | ~0.75× |
| Gemini 2.5 Flash Lite | Google | ~0.75× |
| Amazon Nova Lite | AWS Bedrock | ~0.75× |
| Qwen 3 | AWS Bedrock | ~0.75× |

**When to use FAST:**
- Simple schema questions or quick SQL generation
- Iterating on a schema design across many messages
- Any task where speed matters more than depth

---

### SMART

A step up in reasoning ability, still very cost-efficient. Good for multi-table analysis and moderately complex questions.

| Model | Provider | Relative cost |
|---|---|---|
| Gemini 2.5 Flash | Google | ~3× |
| DeepSeek V3 | AWS Bedrock | ~3.5× |
| Amazon Nova Pro | AWS Bedrock | ~3× |
| Qwen 3 Coder | AWS Bedrock | ~3× |
| Mistral Magistral | AWS Bedrock | ~3.5× |

**When to use SMART:**
- Multi-table JOIN queries with 3–5 tables
- Schema analysis with moderate complexity
- Coding-heavy tasks (Qwen Coder is specialized for code)

---

### ADVANCED

High-capability models from the leading providers. Handles complex multi-table schemas, deep reasoning, and large context windows.

| Model | Provider | Relative cost |
|---|---|---|
| GPT-4o | OpenAI | ~10× |
| GPT-4.1 | OpenAI | ~10× |
| Claude Sonnet | Anthropic | ~10× |
| Gemini 2.5 Pro | Google | ~12× |
| Mistral Large | AWS Bedrock | ~10× |

**When to use ADVANCED:**
- Complex multi-table schema design
- Security audits and performance analysis
- Large schemas (50–200 tables)
- When SMART models give incorrect or incomplete results
- Deep reasoning about schema architecture

---

### POWERFUL

The most capable models available. Reserved for the most demanding tasks — very large schemas, complex agentic workflows, or when only the best reasoning will do.

| Model | Provider | Relative cost |
|---|---|---|
| Claude Opus | Anthropic | ~56× |
| Kimi K2 Thinking | AWS Bedrock | ~28× |

**When to use POWERFUL:**
- Extremely large schemas (200+ tables)
- Full-schema refactoring across many files
- Complex multi-step migration planning
- When ADVANCED models are not getting it right
- Deep security or performance audits on production schemas

> POWERFUL models are significantly more expensive. Use them selectively — most tasks are handled well by ADVANCED or even SMART.

---

## Switching Models

The model selector is in the chat footer of both the SQL Editor and Data Explorer AI panels. You can switch models at any point — the change takes effect on the next message you send. Switching mid-conversation is fully supported.

![The Resona AI model selector footer showing the active model name and credit multiplier with a dropdown to switch](/images/resona-ai/model2.png)

The footer shows the active model name and its credit multiplier (e.g. `Claude Sonnet 10×`). This tells you how many credits the next message will consume relative to the base rate.

---

## Credit System

Credits are the unit of AI usage in Schema Weaver. Every message you send to Resona consumes credits based on the model you selected and the amount of text processed (input + output tokens).

### What Costs Credits

| Action | Credit cost |
|---|---|
| Sending a message to Resona | Yes — based on model × tokens |
| Follow-up messages in the same conversation | Yes |
| Re-running a saved query (no AI) | No |
| Viewing query history | No |
| Starting a new conversation | No |
| Schema indexing | No |

### Credit Multipliers

Credits are calculated per token processed, scaled by the model's tier multiplier. The exact cost per message depends on the length of your question, the schema context included, and the length of the response — but relative costs between models follow the tier multipliers shown above.

A longer schema context means more input tokens and therefore more credits per message, regardless of model.

---

## Plans

Credits are shared between the SQL Editor AI and the Data Explorer AI. Usage in either surface draws from the same monthly pool.

| Plan | Monthly credits | Daily limit | Models available |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Free** | 5,000 | 500 | FAST only |
| **Starter** | 100,000 | 10,000 | FAST + SMART |
| **Pro** | 200,000 | 20,000 | FAST + SMART + ADVANCED |
| **Power** | 433,000 | 43,300 | All tiers (FAST → POWERFUL) |

Credits reset on the 1st of every calendar month. Unused credits do not roll over to the next month.

The **daily limit** is 10% of the monthly allowance — this prevents the full monthly budget from being consumed in a single day.

### What You Can Do With Each Plan

**Free (5,000 credits/month):**
- ~5,000 messages at FAST model rates
- Good for occasional schema questions and exploration
- Only FAST tier models available

**Starter (100,000 credits/month):**
- ~100,000 messages at FAST rates or ~28,000 at SMART rates
- Suitable for regular daily use with SMART models
- Access to FAST and SMART tier models

**Pro (200,000 credits/month):**
- ~200,000 messages at FAST rates or ~20,000 at ADVANCED rates
- Full access to ADVANCED models including GPT-4o and Claude Sonnet
- Recommended for professional schema work and data analysis

**Power (433,000 credits/month):**
- Access to all models including POWERFUL tier (Claude Opus, Kimi K2 Thinking)
- For teams or individuals doing large-scale schema work or intensive data analysis

---

## Checking Usage

Go to **Settings → Usage** to see a breakdown of credits used this month, remaining credits, per-model usage, and daily consumption.

![The Resona AI usage dashboard showing monthly credits used, remaining, per-model breakdown, and daily consumption chart](/images/resona-ai/usage.png)

On team plans, admins can view per-member usage breakdowns and optionally set per-member credit limits.

---

## Credit Top-ups

If you reach your monthly limit before the cycle resets, you can purchase additional credits from **Settings → Billing → Add Credits**. Top-up credits are consumed first (before your monthly allocation) and do not expire at month-end.

---

## FAQ

**Are credits shared between SQL Editor and Data Explorer?**
Yes. Both surfaces draw from the same monthly credit pool on your account.

**What happens when I run out of credits?**
A warning appears at 80% usage. At 100%, Resona falls back to FAST-tier models only (or stops, depending on your plan setting). You can purchase top-up credits or wait for the monthly reset.

**Can multiple team members share a credit pool?**
Yes. On Pro and Power plans, credits are pooled across the workspace. Team admins can set per-user limits in **Settings → Team → Credit Limits**.

**Can I use my own API keys?**
Yes. On Pro and Power plans you can provide your own OpenAI or Anthropic API keys (**Settings → Resona AI → API Keys**). BYOK queries are billed directly to your provider account and do not consume Schema Weaver credits.

**What is the context window?**
Each model has its own context window — the amount of text (schema context + conversation history + your question) it can process at once. For large schemas, models with larger windows handle more tables in a single message. Claude models support 200K tokens; GPT-4o supports 128K; Gemini 2.5 Pro supports 1M tokens.

---

## Next Steps

- [Resona AI Overview](/resona-ai) — what Resona is and how the two surfaces differ
- [AI Chat Guide](/resona-ai/ai-chat) — writing effective prompts for both surfaces
- [SQL Editor AI](/sql-editor/ai-assistant) — the full 55-tool agentic assistant for schema design
- [Data Explorer AI](/data-explorer/ai-overview) — live data analysis, charts, and automated reports

frontmatter.title: Team Collaboration
frontmatter.description: Create teams, invite members, connect projects, and share database access — all managed from Settings → Collaboration in Schema Weaver

team-collaboration.content:
Team Collaboration¶

Schema Weaver's collaboration model is built around Teams. You create a team, invite members by email, and connect your projects to that team. Everyone on the team gets access to the connected projects — and the database connections linked to those projects — without any credential sharing.

Quick links: Open SQL Editor · Open Data Explorer

Where to Find Team Settings¶

All team management is done from the Settings panel:

  1. Click your profile or the settings icon in the SQL Editor
  2. Open Settings → Collaboration

This opens the Teams & Collaboration panel where you can create teams, add members, and connect projects.

Creating a Team¶

Click + New Team in the top-right corner of the Teams & Collaboration panel. Give your team a name and confirm. The team is created immediately and you are set as its Owner.

The Active Team card shows your current team and your role within it. If you belong to multiple teams, use the dropdown to switch between them.

Your Access Level¶

The Your access level card shows your current role in the active team.

Role Privileges
Owner Full management — invite and remove members, connect and disconnect projects, manage all settings
Member Access to connected projects based on project permissions

As the Owner you see the full management interface. Members see the projects they have been given access to.

Adding Team Members¶

Use the Add Teammate section to invite people to your team:

  1. Enter the person's email address in the name@company.com field
  2. Choose their role from the dropdown — member is the default
  3. Click Invite

The person receives an email invitation with a link to join. If they already have a Schema Weaver account, they are added immediately upon accepting. If not, the invitation prompts them to create an account first — they do not need an existing account to receive an invite.

Note: Users can create an account after receiving the invitation.

Team Members List¶

The Team Members section (ACCESS CONTROL) shows everyone currently on the team, with their email address and role badge.

Element Description
Profile photo The member's account avatar
Name and email Display name and email address
YOU badge Marks your own entry in the list
OWNER badge Highlights the team owner
MEMBER badge Standard member role
Delete icon Remove this member from the team (Owner only)

To remove a member, click the trash icon next to their name. Their access is revoked immediately.

Connected Projects¶

The Connected Projects section (PROJECT ACCESS) shows which of your workspace projects are attached to this team. Every project listed here is accessible to all team members.

To connect a project to the team, open the project in the SQL Editor and link it to the team from the project settings. Once connected, it appears in the Connected Projects list and becomes available to all team members.

Team members can open connected projects in the SQL Editor to view and edit schema files, and in the Data Explorer to browse and query live data — all using the project's shared database connection.

Shared Database Connections¶

When a project has a linked PostgreSQL connection, that connection is automatically available to all team members who have access to that project — in both the SQL Editor and the Data Explorer. Nobody needs to enter the database credentials manually.

Action Owner Member
Browse tables in Data Explorer ✅ ✅
Run queries in Data Explorer ✅ ✅
View column statistics ✅ ✅
Export data ✅ ✅
Use Resona AI on live data ✅ ✅
Preview migration (sw push) ✅ ✅
Apply migration (sw push --apply) ✅ ✅
Roll back a migration ✅ ✅
View or change connection credentials ❌ Owner only ❌ Never

Database credentials (host, username, password) are stored encrypted and are never displayed to any team member after the connection is saved — including the person who created it.

What Team Members See¶

Once added to a team and granted access to a project, a team member can:

In the SQL Editor:

  • Open all SQL files in the connected project
  • View the full ER diagram and Schema Compiler results
  • Run sw diff and sw push to review and apply migrations
  • See the full migration history for the project's database

In the Data Explorer:

  • Select the project's shared connection from the connection dropdown
  • Browse all tables, run queries, and view column statistics
  • Use Resona AI to analyze data and generate reports

Audit Trail¶

Every change made through Schema Weaver is attributed to the person who made it:

Event What is recorded
File saved User, timestamp, file, version
File deleted User, timestamp, file
Member invited Inviting user, invited email, role
Migration applied User, timestamp, DDL summary
Migration rolled back User, timestamp, migration reference

The full migration history (visible in the Connection Dashboard) shows who applied each migration and when — giving teams a complete audit trail for compliance and incident review.

Security¶

  • Credentials encrypted at rest — AES-256 encryption for all database passwords
  • Never returned by the API — credentials cannot be exported or retrieved after saving
  • Access enforced at two levels — API middleware validates every request against the user's role; database-level RLS policies enforce row access at the data layer

Version¶

Schema Weaver v1.0

Next Steps¶

  • Database — connect a PostgreSQL database to your project
  • SQL Editor — work on schema files and migrations
  • Data Explorer — browse and query data with shared connections

# Team Collaboration

Schema Weaver's collaboration model is built around **Teams**. You create a team, invite members by email, and connect your projects to that team. Everyone on the team gets access to the connected projects — and the database connections linked to those projects — without any credential sharing.

> Quick links: [Open SQL Editor](https://sql.schemaweaver.vivekmind.com/) · [Open Data Explorer](https://data.schemaweaver.vivekmind.com/)

---

## Where to Find Team Settings

All team management is done from the **Settings** panel:

1. Click your profile or the settings icon in the SQL Editor
2. Open **Settings → Collaboration**

This opens the **Teams & Collaboration** panel where you can create teams, add members, and connect projects.

![The Teams & Collaboration settings panel showing Active Team, access level, Add Teammate form, and Connected Projects](/images/sql-editor/team-colbartion.png)

---

## Creating a Team

Click **+ New Team** in the top-right corner of the Teams & Collaboration panel. Give your team a name and confirm. The team is created immediately and you are set as its **Owner**.

The **Active Team** card shows your current team and your role within it. If you belong to multiple teams, use the dropdown to switch between them.

---

## Your Access Level

The **Your access level** card shows your current role in the active team.

| Role | Privileges |
|---|---|
| **Owner** | Full management — invite and remove members, connect and disconnect projects, manage all settings |
| **Member** | Access to connected projects based on project permissions |

As the Owner you see the full management interface. Members see the projects they have been given access to.

---

## Adding Team Members

Use the **Add Teammate** section to invite people to your team:

1. Enter the person's email address in the **name@company.com** field
2. Choose their role from the dropdown — **member** is the default
3. Click **Invite**

The person receives an email invitation with a link to join. If they already have a Schema Weaver account, they are added immediately upon accepting. If not, the invitation prompts them to create an account first — they do not need an existing account to receive an invite.

> **Note:** Users can create an account after receiving the invitation.

---

## Team Members List

The **Team Members** section (ACCESS CONTROL) shows everyone currently on the team, with their email address and role badge.

![The Team Members list and Connected Projects section showing member roles and project access](/images/sql-editor/team-memebers-project.png)

| Element | Description |
|---|---|
| **Profile photo** | The member's account avatar |
| **Name and email** | Display name and email address |
| **YOU badge** | Marks your own entry in the list |
| **OWNER badge** | Highlights the team owner |
| **MEMBER badge** | Standard member role |
| **Delete icon** | Remove this member from the team (Owner only) |

To remove a member, click the trash icon next to their name. Their access is revoked immediately.

---

## Connected Projects

The **Connected Projects** section (PROJECT ACCESS) shows which of your workspace projects are attached to this team. Every project listed here is accessible to all team members.

To connect a project to the team, open the project in the SQL Editor and link it to the team from the project settings. Once connected, it appears in the Connected Projects list and becomes available to all team members.

Team members can open connected projects in the SQL Editor to view and edit schema files, and in the Data Explorer to browse and query live data — all using the project's shared database connection.

---

## Shared Database Connections

When a project has a linked [PostgreSQL connection](/database/adding-a-connection), that connection is automatically available to all team members who have access to that project — in both the SQL Editor and the Data Explorer. Nobody needs to enter the database credentials manually.

| Action | Owner | Member |
|---|---|---|
| Browse tables in Data Explorer | ✅ | ✅ |
| Run queries in Data Explorer | ✅ | ✅ |
| View column statistics | ✅ | ✅ |
| Export data | ✅ | ✅ |
| Use Resona AI on live data | ✅ | ✅ |
| Preview migration (sw push) | ✅ | ✅ |
| Apply migration (sw push --apply) | ✅ | ✅ |
| Roll back a migration | ✅ | ✅ |
| View or change connection credentials | ❌ Owner only | ❌ Never |

Database credentials (host, username, password) are stored encrypted and are never displayed to any team member after the connection is saved — including the person who created it.

---

## What Team Members See

Once added to a team and granted access to a project, a team member can:

**In the SQL Editor:**
- Open all SQL files in the connected project
- View the full ER diagram and Schema Compiler results
- Run `sw diff` and `sw push` to review and apply migrations
- See the full migration history for the project's database

**In the Data Explorer:**
- Select the project's shared connection from the connection dropdown
- Browse all tables, run queries, and view column statistics
- Use Resona AI to analyze data and generate reports

---

## Audit Trail

Every change made through Schema Weaver is attributed to the person who made it:

| Event | What is recorded |
|---|---|
| File saved | User, timestamp, file, version |
| File deleted | User, timestamp, file |
| Member invited | Inviting user, invited email, role |
| Migration applied | User, timestamp, DDL summary |
| Migration rolled back | User, timestamp, migration reference |

The full migration history (visible in the [Connection Dashboard](/database/connection-dashboard)) shows who applied each migration and when — giving teams a complete audit trail for compliance and incident review.

---

## Security

- **Credentials encrypted at rest** — AES-256 encryption for all database passwords
- **Never returned by the API** — credentials cannot be exported or retrieved after saving
- **Access enforced at two levels** — API middleware validates every request against the user's role; database-level RLS policies enforce row access at the data layer

---

## Version

Schema Weaver v1.0

---

## Next Steps

- [Database](/database) — connect a PostgreSQL database to your project
- [SQL Editor](/sql-editor) — work on schema files and migrations
- [Data Explorer](/data-explorer) — browse and query data with shared connections
